questions.question: A cylinder has radius 4 cm and height 9 cm. What is its volume in cubic centimeters? (Use π ≈ 3.14 or leave your answer in terms of π.)
questions.explanation: Volume of cylinder = πr²h = π(4)²(9) = 16π · 9 = 144π cm³. Using π ≈ 3.14 gives approximately 452.16 cm³.

questions.question: A rectangular prism has dimensions 5 m by 3 m by 8 m. A smaller rectangular prism with dimensions 2 m by 3 m by 4 m is removed from it. What is the remaining volume?
questions.explanation: Volume of large prism = 5 × 3 × 8 = 120 m³. Volume of small prism = 2 × 3 × 4 = 24 m³. Remaining volume = 120 − 24 = 96 m³.

questions.question: A cone has a base radius of 6 inches and a height of 10 inches. Express its volume in terms of π.
A sphere has a diameter of 12 cm. What is its volume in terms of π?
questions.explanation: If diameter = 12 cm, then radius = 6 cm. Volume of sphere = (4/3)πr³ = (4/3)π(6)³ = (4/3)π(216) = 288π cm³.

questions.question: A triangular prism has a triangular base with base 8 ft and height 5 ft. The prism extends 12 ft in depth. What is its volume?
questions.explanation: Area of triangular base = (1/2)(8)(5) = 20 ft². Volume of prism = base area × depth = 20 × 12 = 240 ft³.

questions.question: Two cylinders have the same height of 7 cm. Cylinder A has radius 3 cm and Cylinder B has radius 6 cm. How many times larger is the volume of Cylinder B than Cylinder A?
questions.explanation: Volume A = π(3)²(7) = 63π. Volume B = π(6)²(7) = 252π. Ratio = 252π / 63π = 4. The radius doubled, so volume increased by 2² = 4.
